prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a small robot naps in an armchair while a thought bubble above it contains a tiny floating miniature world of hills, paths, and a goal flag, and the robot rehearses walking through that imaginary world without leaving its chair. Style friendly like an XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A robot dozing in a chair imagines a tiny world inside its thought bubble and practices a route there, showing that a world model lets an agent plan by imagining instead of acting.
caption: A world model lets an agent rehearse the future in its head, trying out futures that never cost a single real step.

after_heading: 2. The Four Components and Their Objective
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: four cartoon hands cooperate around a single glowing orb of latent state, one hand squeezing raw pictures into the orb, one hand nudging the orb forward in time, one hand reading a reward gem off it, one hand reconstructing a picture back out of it. Style friendly like a Kurzgesagt panel, expressive, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Four hands work around one glowing latent state orb, one encoding observations in, one advancing it forward, one reading reward, one decoding observations back out, showing the four cooperating components of a world model.
caption: A world model is four jobs sharing one compact state: encode what you see, predict what comes next, guess the reward, and reconstruct the picture.

prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a robot athlete sprints on a treadmill inside a soft dreamy bubble, the scenery scrolling past is clearly imagined and slightly cloud-like, and the robot gets visibly fitter and faster while never actually moving forward. Style friendly like an XKCD panel, expressive body language, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A robot trains hard on a treadmill inside a dreamlike bubble, improving its running without moving anywhere, illustrating how Dreamer learns behavior from imagined rollouts instead of real environment steps.
caption: Dreamer trains its policy entirely on imagined rollouts, getting better at the real task without spending real experience to do it.

after_heading: 2. The MCTS Algorithm: Selection, Expansion, Evaluation, Backup
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a tiny explorer stands at the root of a branching tree of glowing paths, sends a scout down one promising branch, the scout peeks at what lies ahead, then runs a thread of light back up the branch making the chosen path glow brighter for next time. Style friendly like a Kurzgesagt panel, expressive, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: An explorer at the base of a branching tree sends a scout down one branch to peek ahead and carries a glowing value back up to brighten that path, depicting the select, expand, evaluate, backup loop of Monte Carlo Tree Search.
caption: Monte Carlo Tree Search grows knowledge one scouted branch at a time, letting each lookahead brighten the paths worth revisiting.

after_heading: 3. MuZero's Learned Latent Model: Representation, Dynamics, Prediction
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a confident robot plays a board game brilliantly while wearing a blindfold over the actual rulebook, instead consulting its own private imagined game pieces floating beside its head that it invented to predict outcomes. Style friendly like an XKCD panel, expressive and knowing, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A blindfolded robot plays a board game expertly by consulting its own invented imaginary pieces rather than the real rules, illustrating how MuZero plans with a learned latent model instead of a given simulator.
caption: MuZero never gets told the rules of the game; it invents its own private model of consequences and plans inside that.

after_heading: 1. The Long-Horizon Problem: Compounding Error and Exploding Search
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a line of robots whispers a prediction down the chain like a game of telephone, the first holding a crisp tidy shape, each next robot holding a slightly more wobbly smudged version, the last robot proudly holding a hopeless blob that looks nothing like the original. Style friendly like an XKCD panel, expressive, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A row of robots passes a prediction down a line like telephone, each version more distorted until the last holds an unrecognizable blob, illustrating how small per-step model errors compound over long horizons.
caption: Predict far enough ahead and tiny per-step errors snowball, which is exactly why long horizons demand temporal abstraction over step-by-step rollouts.
